task_updates.executor_notes: Implemented `src/shared/state/overlayStack.ts` as the centralized ordered overlay store. The public API now exposes JSDoc-documented open/update/close actions plus topmost, topmost-modal, layer, and element-membership selectors and matching hooks. Global ownership is centralized: the first modal overlay snapshots `document.body.style.pointerEvents`, any open modal keeps it at `'none'`, and the last modal restore returns the prior inline value regardless of close order. Modal focus restoration only fires when the closing overlay was the current topmost modal, so nested modal closes restore in reverse close order while non-topmost closes do not steal focus. Added focused store tests covering pointer-events cleanup, reverse-order focus restore, layer lookup, and element-membership queries.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. `overlayStack.ts` now exists with documented public actions/selectors/hooks, imperative topmost and layer APIs, reverse-order modal focus restoration, and body pointer-events cleanup derived from modal presence rather than close order.
